problem_65.natural_language: An ideal is radical iff the quotient ring is reduced.
problem_65.lean:
theorem ideal_isRadical_iff_quotient_isReduced
    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) :
    I.IsRadical ↔ IsReduced (R ⧸ I)
problem_65.natural_language_proof:
Proof: write `π : R → R ⧸ I` for the quotient map. Suppose `I` is radical. If `x : R ⧸ I` is
nilpotent, say `x^n = 0`, pick a representative `r` with `x = π r`. Then `0 = x^n = π (r^n)`, so
`r^n ∈ I`. Since `I` is radical, `r ∈ I`, hence `x = 0`. Thus `R ⧸ I` has no nonzero nilpotents,
so it is reduced.

Conversely, assume `R ⧸ I` is reduced. If `r^n ∈ I`, then `π(r)^n = 0` in the quotient, so by
reducedness `π(r) = 0`, i.e. `r ∈ I`. This is exactly that `I` is radical.
